_comment_purpose: Secondary als-dimmer instance for the dual-display compare rig: a Pi4 driving two 12.3" displays through an HDMI splitter, where one panel uses local-dimming (controlled by the primary als-dimmer instance, e.g. config_fpga_opti4001_dimmer2048_12_3_nq1v1.json on the default ports/sockets) and the other is a BOE edge-lit panel driven via the i2c-tiny-usb-pwm dongle. The primary owns the ALS sensor and runs normally; this secondary instance has NO sensor of its own (sensor.type=null) and boots straight into MANUAL mode. The external compare app starts this secondary on demand (systemctl start als-dimmer-pwm.service), commands absolute-brightness in nits on both daemons via their socket APIs to equalize the two displays, then stops it (systemctl stop) when compare mode is exited. Reuses the boe_i2c_pwm output stack and its calibration LUT verbatim — only the control plane differs from config_opti4001_boe_i2c_pwm.json.
_comment_diffs_vs_single_mode: Differences vs config_opti4001_boe_i2c_pwm.json: (1) sensor.type=null instead of opti4001 - the BOE edge-lit panel doesn't get its own ALS, the external app handles synchronization; (2) tcp_socket.listen_port=9001 and unix_socket.path=/tmp/als-dimmer-pwm.sock to avoid colliding with the primary instance on 9000 / /tmp/als-dimmer.sock; (3) state_file=/tmp/als-dimmer-pwm-state.json for the same reason; (4) notification.enabled=false because the streamdeck UI is wired to the primary instance only - dual-display compare is driven from the external app. Output, brightness_to_nits, zones, thermal_compensation are identical to the single-mode i2c_pwm config.
